programme.title: Tom Read Wilson's Magnificent Journey
programme.desc: This series reimagines the rite of passage once undertaken by 17th, 18th and 19th-century aristocrats on their Grand Tours of Europe. Craving a touch of cultural enlightenment, Tom Read Wilson sets out to follow in their footsteps. He begins in Paris, where he's introduced to the refined world of aristocratic etiquette under the guidance of a Parisian contessa, before visiting some of the city's most culturally significant sites including Notre-Dame and the Gardens of Versailles. He then continues his journey south, travelling by horse and carriage, and later by train, to arrive at Lake Annecy, where he checks into a lakeside hotel frequented by the Grand Tourists. Exec Prods: Ros Edwards/Ed Taylor; Dir: Alexis Girardet; Honey Bee Media Ltd (S1 Ep1/3)

programme.title: Jane McDonald: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Jane McDonald's on a mission to step out of her comfort zone, pushing herself beyond her limits to travel to the ends of the earth in search of bucket-list adventures - crossing oceans, continents and time zones from Antarctica to the Arctic. Her journey takes in the Falkland Islands and South Georgia, Uruguay, Brazil, Greece and Italy before finally reaching Norway, Svalbard and the Arctic Circle, travelling by yacht, plane, helicopter, rib boat and even kayak in search of incredible experiences. In this episode - the Antarctic peninsula. It's as close to the South Pole as Jane can get and the beginning of her epic expedition. Jane takes a ride around Winter Island, getting up close with icebergs and the native wildlife; braves the dangerous Antarctic waters in a two person kayak; Jane explores the mountainous glacial terrain of Livingstone Island on foot, coming face to face with elephant seals; and tries aerial yoga. Exec Prods: Kieron Collins/Mark Powell; Dir: Jenny MacLeod; JetSetTV Ltd (Ep1/6)
